Sample from which powder was derived is a gray, medium- to coarse-grained rock composed of quartz, feldspar, and a mafic mineral.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.fine.granit1.spectrum.txt

Powder derived from a gray, medium-grained rock consisting of white feldspar grains, some of which contain red or pinkish cores, quartz, and minor biotite. Some of the feldspar appears to be perthitic.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.fine.granit2.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is a light gray, medium- to coarse-grained rock consisting of gray plagioclase, white feldspar, quartz, and chloritized biotite.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.fine.granit3.spectrum.txt

Powder derived from a pinkish medium-grained sample consisting of feldspar and quartz with minor biotite. Some areas of the sample show granophyric texture, and a few vugs are present.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.fine.granit5.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is reddish, banded, porphyritic rock with the phenocrysts small (< 1 mm) and variable in size.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.felsic.fine.rhyoli1.spectrum.txt

The sample from which the powder was derived consists of a dark gray, fine-grained groundmass, with yellow (hypersthene?), dark green (augite?) and white (plagioclase) phenocrysts. The sample is also slightly vuggy.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.andesi2.spectrum.txt

A dark gray, porphyritic and vuggy rock. The phenocrysts are plagioclase laths, orangish-yellow olivine and opaques.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.andesi4.spectrum.txt

Powder derived from sample: a dark gray, medium-grained rock composed of plagioclase, a dark mafic phase and minor biotite.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.diorite1.spectrum.txt

Powder derived from a dark gray medium-grained rock consisting of gray plagioclase, pinkish potash feldspar, quartz, biotite, scattered sulfides, and a black mafic phase.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.granod1.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is a dark gray medium-grained rock consisting of light gray plagioclase grains, white to pinkish feldspar (plagioclase >> alkali feldspar), green epidote or apatite, a black mafic phase and quartz.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.granod2.spectrum.txt

Sample from which the powder was derived is purplish-gray, medium-grained granular rock composed of feldspar, a greenish to black mafic mineral and opaques.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.monzon1.spectrum.txt

The sample from which the powder was derived is a black homogenous glass with good conchoidal fracture.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.obsidi1.spectrum.txt

Powder derived from light gray medium-grained rock composed of pink feldspar, grayish plagioclase, quartz and biotite.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.qmonzo1.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is dark gray fine-grained rock spotted with white feldspar grains in a dark gray groundmass with scattered mafics, opaques, and sulfides.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.syenit1.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is a medium-grained leucocratic rock showing some foliation, consisting of plagioclase, nepheline and biotite.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.syenit2.spectrum.txt

Sample from which powder was derived is a medium-grained gray rock composed of whitish plagioclase, quartz, biotite, and a black mafic mineral.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.fine.tonali1.spectrum.txt

The sample from which the powder was derived consists of dark gray tabular plagioclase containing interstitial bluish-green granulated feldspar groundmass, and some opaque areas approx. 1-3 mm in size scattered through the sample.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.fine.anorth1.spectrum.txt

Sample from which the powder is derived is a gray, porphyritic, vuggy or vesicular rock with green phenocrysts ranging in size from < 1 mm to 2 mm and making up < 5% of the whole rock.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.fine.basal7.spectrum.txt

The sample from which the powder was derived is greenish-black aphanitic rock.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.fine.basal1.spectrum.txt

The sample from which the powder was derived is light gray porphyritic rock with green olivine phenocrysts 1-2 mm in size making up about 3% of the rock, set in a gray, very fine-grained groundmass.Particle size was 0-75 micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.fine.basal10.spectrum.txt
